programme.title: Trucking Hell: Episode 3
programme.desc: Things are far from simple for the recovery crews this week. When a loaded HGV suffers a puncture on a busy motorway, Crouch recovery op Jim Howard and heavy vehicle mechanic Jack Watson, are called to the scene.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: A digger has fallen off the back of a lorry and rolled over on its side - blocking one side of a busy A road. Richard Crouch and recovery op Lee Brown have a hay bale of a time trying to get a valuable straw load on a broken down trailer back safely to it

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: At Crouch HQ, there is excitement in the air as the team help to make trucking superfan Chris's wedding day extra special. Snod is called to recover two crashed cars from the side of the M69 - the exact stretch of road that his friend was killed on while

programme.title: Trucking Hell: Episode 6
programme.desc: Dave and Adam are under pressure to recover a 100 tonne train engine before it sinks into the soft ground. Jim has to rescue a lorry that has become stuck in a ditch.

programme.title: Trucking Hell: Episode 8
programme.desc: A luxury coach gives up the ghost on a busy motorway slip road. A straightforward job segues into disaster for Marta. Scott races to recover a damaged trailer before dark descends.

programme.title: Trucking Hell: Episode 9
programme.desc: Jim must rescue a 32-tonne truck from a soft dirt embankment. Chris manouevres a skip lorry out of city centre traffic. Marta is driven around the bend trying to deliver a 48-foot articulated bus.

programme.title: Trucking Hell: Episode 10
programme.desc: A recycling plant needs two old portable cabins swapped out for new ones, making for some serious heavy lifting for Nick. Jim sees to an old Toyota abandoned in a live lane on the M1.

programme.title: Trucking Hell: Episode 11
programme.desc: Jim and his team face a difficult foggy morning on the M1 when a crash between two artics causes a five-mile-long tailback.

programme.title: Trucking Hell: Episode 12
programme.desc: Veteran driver Jim attends an emergency call-out to recover a lorry that has broken down bang in the middle of a busy ring road. Motor racing fanatic Merrie has a hunch she may have landed her dream job when she attends a broken down lorry.

programme.title: Trucking Hell: Episode 13
programme.desc: Steve and the team find themselves in deep water when they attempt to recover a car that has crashed into a river. Jim is tasked with recovering a van that has become wedged on top of a garden wall.

programme.title: Trucking Hell: Episode 14
programme.desc: Steve battles rush hour traffic to manoeuvre his recovery vehicle around the narrow roads to get to a 7.5-tonne lorry out of a ditch, while Rory must deliver a dodgy van to a scrapyard for destruction.

programme.title: Trucking Hell: Episode 15
programme.desc: Lantern's only female driver Merrie is tasked with the removal of an abandoned lorry, but it is soon clear this will not be a routine job. As she takes a closer look, she discovers that not only has the lorry been used as a mobile home, but that it is hou

programme.title: Trucking Hell: Episode 16
programme.desc: Rory risks life and limb to recovered a skip lorry from the M1. Jim has a happy reunion with his own truck. Chris has to find a way of separating two HGVs that have somehow become wedged together.

programme.title: Trucking Hell: Episode 17
programme.desc: Jim faces mayhem on the motorway when a massive pile-up blocks the busy M1.

programme.title: Trucking Hell: Episode 18
programme.desc: Marta and Pat have to pull a bin lorry out of a muddy ditch. But first they must battle their way through a thick wall of thorns.

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 12
programme.desc: A powerful storm brings up painful memories of the past - and puts everyone in jeopardy in the present.

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 13
programme.desc: When Sokka and Katara get too sick to travel, Aang goes out on his own to find a cure for them. But he is captured by Zhao and held prisoner inside an impenetrable fortress.

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 14
programme.desc: Aang, Katara, and Sokka discover a village of people who are all reliant on a fortuneteller in their everyday lives but they are so reliant that they are blind to the imminent danger they are all in.

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 15
programme.desc: Aang feels insecure when Katara and Sokka come across something that reminds them of home. Because Aang thinks his friends might leave him, he hides an important message which only leads to trouble.

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 16
programme.desc: Aang attempts to find a Firebending master to teach him while Katara learns that she possesses a special power.

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 17
programme.desc: After hearing rumors about surviving Airbenders, the kids journey to the Northern Air temple to see for themselves. What they find there could spell disaster for all enemies of the Fire Nation.

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 18
programme.desc: The kids reach the Northern Water tribe, and Aang and Katara look for a Waterbending Master as Sokka falls for a princess. Zuko must find a way to continue his quest for the Avatar.

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 19
programme.desc: The Northern Water Tribe braces itself for an attack by Admiral Zhao's Fire Navy fleet. Aang struggles to defend the city. He wonders if there isn't a better way to use his Avatar abilities.

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 20
programme.desc: Zhao's invasion breaks through the outer wall of the Northern Water Tribe's defenses, and he reveals a sinister plan. Aang goes to the spirit world for help.

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 1
programme.desc: Aang and the group meet an Earth Kingdom general who wants to use Aang's powerful `Avatar State' as a weapon to defeat the Fire Nation. But it's more difficult than Aang thinks.

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 2
programme.desc: On the way to Omashu, the kids meet some nomads who show them a shortcut through the `cave of two lovers'. When they get lost in the caves, they must trust in love to escape.

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 3
programme.desc: The kids return to Omashu so that Aang can learn Earthbending from King Bumi; however, when they get there, they are shocked to find the city is now in Fire Nation hands.

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 4
programme.desc: When the kids end up in a mysterious and strange swamp, their fears are exposed. And Aang learns about being `connected to the Earth' from an unlikely teacher.

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 5
programme.desc: Aang and the gang discover an Earth town that celebrates `Avatar Day'. They learn that it is not a celebration of the Avatar, but a festival of anger for something Aang did in his past.

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 6
programme.desc: In search for an Earthbending teacher for Aang, the kids go to an underground Earthbending tournament where they encounter Toph, a blind, but powerful Earthbender.

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 7
programme.desc: Travelling without Uncle now, Zuko wanders alone into an Earth Kingdom town where he bonds with a local boy. Meanwhile, Zuko is haunted by memories of his own youth.

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 8
programme.desc: As the kids are pursued relentlessly by a mysterious machine, their exhaustion puts them at each other's throats.

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 9
programme.desc: When Aang struggles with a block while trying to learn Earthbending from Toph, he wonders if the problem is with him or his teacher. Meanwhile, Sokka gets himself in a jam.

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 10
programme.desc: A professor leads the kids to a spirit library in the middle of the desert, where Sokka hopes to discover powerful secrets to use against the Fire Nation.

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 11
programme.desc: Aang searches for Appa, while Katara struggles to keep everyone together so they can survive in the vast desert. Meanwhile, Uncle and Zuko seek help from an ancient secret society.

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 12
programme.desc: When the kids can't take the easy way into Ba Sing Se, they must escort a family of refugees there the hard way, through the deadly Serpent's Pass.

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 13
programme.desc: Aang discovers a colossal Fire Nation invention heading for Ba Sing Se, and the kids must stop it before it destroys the great wall that protects the city.

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 14
programme.desc: Aang and the kids finally arrive in Ba Sing Se to see the Earth King, only to find mysterious forces within the city conspiring to stop them.

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 15
programme.desc: A series of short stories that explore the individual adventures of Katara and Toph, Uncle, Sokka, Aang, Zuko, and Momo.

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 16
programme.desc: In this special episode, we go back in time to the moment Appa was stolen.

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 17
programme.desc: While searching for Appa, the kids run into Jet but are torn about whether to trust him or not. Meanwhile, Zuko also hunts for Appa.

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 18
programme.desc: The kids battle Long Feng when they try to show the Earth King the conspiracy taking place in his city. Zuko becomes seriously ill and has odd dreams as he faces a spiritual crisis.

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 19
programme.desc: A guru at the Eastern Air Temple helps Aang take the next step in his Avatar journey. Plus, Sokka meets his long-lost father, Azula hatches a diabolical plan, and Uncle and Zuko open a tea shop.

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 20
programme.desc: Everyone races against time to stop Azula's sinister conquest of Ba Sing Se. Zuko and Katara spend time together, while Aang's destiny hangs in the balance, as does Zuko's.

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 1
programme.desc: After sustaining serious injuries, Aang awakens to find himself aboard a Fire Nation ship. Meanwhile, Zuko journeys home.

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 2
programme.desc: To better camouflage themselves as real Fire Nation citizens, the kids check out a Fire Nation school. Also, Zuko confronts Uncle.

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 3
programme.desc: The gang arrive at a suffering Fire Nation fishing village, and a mysterious spirit appears to help the villagers.

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 4
programme.desc: Sokka feels that he is not contributing enough to the group, and searches for a mysterious master to teach him the ways of the sword.

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 5
programme.desc: Zuko, Azula, Mai, and Ty Lee go on vacation at Lo and Li's beach house, where they learn a lot about themselves and each other. Meanwhile, the kids face a new enemy.

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 6
programme.desc: Aang and Zuko are taken on parallel adventures that give them insight into their forefathers' pasts, but how does the tale of Roku and Sozin matter to them now?

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 7
programme.desc: When Toph discovers a quick way to make cash, Katara disapproves, and the rift between them has disastrous consequences.

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 8
programme.desc: In a spooky village, Katara makes a special connection; the group investigate strange disappearances that occur during full moons.

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 9
programme.desc: On the eve of the eclipse, Aang's worry overtakes him and his dreams become nightmares. Before long, he can't tell the difference between what's real and what's not.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Get Shredder!
programme.desc: Shredder & Krang are still loose on Earth, but without the resources of the Technodrome, they plan to steal equipment from the abandoned Hall of Science at the World Fair.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Wrath of the Rat King
programme.desc: Burne has created a hot line for callers to use should they spot those responsible for the destruction of the Channel Six News Building. Inside the Hall of Science, Shredder is fearful that the Turtles will eventually find a way into the building.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Cyber-Turtles
programme.desc: While investigating the theft of a rare telescope, the Astro-Viewer, the Turtles discover odd tracks leading away from the National Space Exploration Centre.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): State of Shock
programme.desc: After defeating a mysterious group of ninjas at the Emeryville Power Station, the Turtles decode part of a map that they were able to liberate from the would-be thieves.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Cry H.A.V.O.C.!
programme.desc: The Turtles get the drop on masked thieves hijacking a jewellery store, only to have themselves arrested for the crime! Before the turtles can be booked, they are rescued by the mutants Amok and Overdrive.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): H.A.V.O.C. in the Streets!
programme.desc: Titanus sends of couple of his H.A.V.O.C.mutants to bust into the aerospace plant and steal some much needed equipment. Only problem is that the Turtles were waiting for them.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Enter: Krakus
programme.desc: Krakus, a cop from the future, arrives and thinking that the Turtles are in league with Titanus, shakes them down for information.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Turtle Trek
programme.desc: Krang activates his new dimensional portal, thanks to Shredder's installation of the Energy Transmitter at the Power Relay Station, giving him access to Dimension X.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): The Unknown Ninja
programme.desc: While watching video tapes of old battles, Raphael sees a mystery person spying on them. While the Turtles investigate, an alien warlord named Dregg sends his TechnoGansters to steal all the gold on Earth.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Dregg of the Earth
programme.desc: April contacts the Turtles to let them know that Dregg has landed on Earth and is preparing a press conference. The Turtles rush to meet April at the Park, where they hear Dregg announce his intentions and offers the world his molecular converter.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): The Wrath of Medusa
programme.desc: Believing that the Turtles are the only thing that stands between him and total domination of the planet, Dregg makes contact with Medusa, a sinisterly beautiful alien.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): The Showdown
programme.desc: April contacts the Turtles to inform them that all the TV stations in the City are jammed. Sleazebug tries to recruit the Turtles for a martial arts tournament.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Split-Second
programme.desc: Carter, April and the Turtles investigate a break-in at the Antique Timepiece Museum. Convinced that Dregg is responsible, the Turtles discover a clue on a wall display.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Carter the Enforcer
programme.desc: Splinter convinces the Turtles that it is their duty to fight evil, even when the whole world is against them. Carter and the Turtles team up with April to get the goods on Dregg.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): The Return of Dregg
programme.desc: While the Turtles work on using fragments of the Vortex Crystal to stabilize their mutations, Lord Dregg plans to use the fragments to create another Vortex Crystal to power his Vortex Transporter.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): The Beginning of the End
programme.desc: While Raphael, Michelangelo, Donatello and Carter work on restoring Leonardo to his regular self, Mung steals all the plutonium from a power plant in order for Dregg to build a Plutonium Ray to turn Leonardo into a radioactive killer.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): The Power of Three
programme.desc: Mungg informs Dregg that he came across two individuals who claimed they were old acquaintances of the turtles: Shredder and Krang!

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): A Turtle in Time
programme.desc: The Turtles', Shredder's and Krang's battles skills, powers, and memories are absorbed into Dregg.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Turtles to the Second Power
programme.desc: Dregg and his crew plan an invasion of Earth to make it his new home base, but first he has to get his transporter chip back from Shredder.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): The Day the Earth Disappeared
programme.desc: With the city breaking apart, the Turtles head to the street to investigate and see the Dreggnaut appear in the sky. With Mung's help, Dregg reprograms the transporter to open a warp in space.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Divide and Conquer
programme.desc: Lord Dregg launches a final attack on the Ninja Turtles.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Turtle Tracks
programme.desc: April O'Neil, ace reporter for Channel 6, investigates recent break-ins, allegedly by Ninjas, which have resulted in the thefts of numerous technologies.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Enter: The Shredder
programme.desc: The Shredder kidnaps Splinter in an attempt to lure the Turtles to his own cause. In order to rescue Splinter the Turtles must enter the Shredder's mobile fortress and brave its dangerous weaponry.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): A Thing about Rats
programme.desc: Baxter Stockman, a mad scientist, is showing an exterminator his new invention: a rat catching robot called Mousers.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Hot Rodding Teenagers from Dimension X
programme.desc: Shredder experiments with the Inter-Dimensional Portal, hoping to contact Krang's' Rock Soldiers and prepare an invasion of Earth.
programme.sub-title: Hot Rodding Teenagers from Dimension X

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Shredder & Splintered
programme.desc: Shredder lures Splinter into the Technodrome, this time with the promise that a weapon in his possession can restore the sensei to human form.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Return of the Shredder
programme.desc: Shredder trains students at the Dojo for a crooked Turtles gang. The mock turtles' crime spree starts turning the public against Michelangelo, Raphael, Donatello and Leonardo.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): The Incredible Shrinking Turtles
programme.desc: In the first of a four-parter, the amazing amphibians search for the segments of the powerful crystal Eye of Sarnoth. Shredder cuts the Turtles down to size, literally.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): It Came from Beneath the Sewers
programme.desc: In the second of a four-parter about the pursuit of the Eye of Sarnoth, the Turtles are attacked by a huge alien plant that is making New York into a jungle.
programme.sub-title: It Came from Beneath the Sewers

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): The Mean Machines
programme.desc: The hunt for the Eye of Sarnoth continues in the third of a four-parter with the Turtles in Groucho-like disguises. Shredder hooks up a fragment of the Eye to the computer Omnus.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Curse of the Evil Eye
programme.desc: Shredder is in control of the Eye of Sarnoth, enabling him to transmute matter. Shredder brings to life a dragon which soars through the air with shocked shellbacks Leonardo and Michelangelo aboard.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Case of the Killer Pizzas
programme.desc: The Turtles' favourite food, pizza, is also the source of their problem. Nasty, fanged creatures have been planted in pizza boxes! When the critters crack open their chrysalises, they become enormous.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Enter: The Fly
programme.desc: Shredder sends April a rare duko plant that renders her unconscious. The Turtles seek a rare leaf that can serve as an antidote.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Invasion of the Punk Frogs
programme.desc: Mutagen, sent by Krang from Dimension X, changes four Florida frogs into half-human creatures four and a half feet tall. Shredder, training them in his evil ways, names them Attila the Frog, Genghis Frog, Rasputin the Mad Frog and Napoleon Bonafrog.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Splinter No More
programme.desc: Michelangelo senses that their mentor is "mondo depressed" and Donatello finds a way of changing back Splinter the rat to Yoshi the man.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): New York's Shiniest
programme.desc: Shredder taps into New York's computers to steal plans for robots and creates an army of robots to destroy the Turtles and loot the city.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Teenagers from Dimension X
programme.desc: Neutrino teens Kala, Zak and Eask arrive on Earth and alert the Turtles to Shredder's latest plan to make "mindless zombies" of the Turtles.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): The Catwoman from Channel 6
programme.desc: A matter transporter results in strange happenings including TV reporter April O'Neil being put into a cat-like state and a tiger chasing the Turtles and Splinter, their mentor-rat.

programme.title: Weird Or What? With William Shatner: Life After Death
programme.desc: Life After Death: Scientists in England conduct groundbreaking séances, while someone returns from the grave.

programme.title: Weird Or What? With William Shatner: Monsters
programme.desc: Monsters: A fisherman uncovers the remains of an unidentified sea creature on a beach, and a hairy creature causes panic in New Delhi.

programme.title: Weird Or What? With William Shatner: The End Of The World
programme.desc: The End of the World: Decoding Mayan texts, a physicist predicts a cataclysm will destroy the world. A NASA scientist makes an alien discovery.

programme.title: Weird Or What? With William Shatner: Parallel Worlds
programme.desc: Parallel Worlds: While hiking, a woman has a terrifying encounter with an entity from another world.

programme.title: Weird Or What? With William Shatner: Freaks Of Nature
programme.desc: Freaks Of Nature: A small community is devastated by an earth-shattering 'boom,' while an paranormal fog seems to alter the very fabric of time.

programme.title: Weird Or What? With William Shatner: Paranormal Mysteries
programme.desc: Paranormal Mysteries: A woman is terrorised by an invisible attacker and a mother undergoes a violent and demonic transformation.

programme.title: Weird Or What? With William Shatner: Medical Mysteries
programme.desc: Medical Mysteries: An adrenaline junkie and her unborn baby somehow survive when their parachute fails, and a firefighter is incinerated in his bed.

programme.title: Weird Or What? With William Shatner: Power Of The Mind
programme.desc: Power of the Mind: A housewife has a strange and sudden vision that helps to solve a murder. A man remembers every single moment of his life.

programme.title: Weird Or What? With William Shatner: Ancient Mysteries
programme.desc: Ancient Mysteries: A girl finds a 1000-year-old skull that may prove aliens once walked the planet, and ancient stone carvings could rewrite the story of evolution.

programme.title: Weird Or What? With William Shatner: Amazing Survival
programme.desc: Amazing Survival: Incredible stories of people who cheated death, including a man who escaped from the avalanche of debris caused by the collapse of the World Trade Center.

programme.title: Weird Or What? With William Shatner: Man Beasts
programme.desc: Man Beasts: We're all fascinated by mysteries. But is the unexplained really unexplainable? William Shatner investigates in an attempt to find a logical explanation.

programme.title: Weird Or What? With William Shatner: Aliens Walk Among Us
programme.desc: Stories that suggest aliens are among us, including an Arizona woman who claims to have been fathered by an alien in a top-secret government experiment.

programme.title: Weird Or What? With William Shatner: Curses
programme.desc: Curses: Do cursed objects really exist? This episode explores a painting in Britain that is said to start deadly house fires, a sinister doll in Florida and a curse-proof house.

programme.title: Weird Or What? With William Shatner: Weird Animal Behaviour
programme.desc: Weird Animal Behaviour: Three extraordinary stories about the supersensory power of animals, including a psychic octopus that correctly predicted multiple World Cup winners.

programme.title: Weird Or What? With William Shatner: Ghosts In The Machine
programme.desc: Ghosts in the Machine: Three tales of everyday objects that are haunted by the dead, including the story of a man who is attacked in bed by an invisible force.

programme.title: Weird Or What? With William Shatner: Premonitions
programme.desc: Premonitions: A look into the predictive power of the subconscious, including a man who warns of a plane crash before it happens following a series of vivid nightmares.

programme.title: Weird Or What? With William Shatner: Mysterious Vanishings
programme.desc: Mysterious Vanishings: Baffling stories of people who vanished without a trace, including a young Tasmanian pilot who is chased by unidentified aircraft before disappearing.

programme.title: Weird Or What? With William Shatner
programme.desc: Medical Mysteries: Medics at a hospital fall gravely ill after treating a mysteriously ailing patient in a circumstance that can't be explained by science. Or can it?

programme.title: Weird Or What? With William Shatner: Mind Control
programme.desc: Mind Control: Three spine-tingling stories, including a seance that turns a housewife into a literary genius as she channels a spirit to write a series of acclaimed books.

programme.title: Weird Or What? With William Shatner: Falling Man, Space Rocks, Missing Feet
programme.desc: ...With William Shatner. Falling Man, Space Rocks, Missing Feet: A high-rise window washer somehow survives a 50-storey fall and ball-sized ice chunks fall from the sky.
programme.sub-title: Falling Man, Space Rocks, Missing Feet

programme.title: Kim's Convenience: Who's Pranking Who?
programme.desc: Kimchee's plan to impress the higher-ups backfires. Shannon inadvertently insults Umma's cooking. Appa and Janet enter a prank war.

programme.title: Kim's Convenience: Matchy Matchy
programme.desc: Umma tries to set up Janet with their new mason. Shannon encourages Jung to give Appa a gift. Kimchee and Shannon work on employee evaluations.

programme.title: Kim's Convenience: Hugs & Prayers
programme.desc: Janet and Appa take a first aid course. Umma worries her prayers cause more harm than good. Shannon and Jung discuss their relationship.

programme.title: Kim's Convenience: Friends and Family
programme.desc: Jung gives Appa and Umma a business presentation for the store. Shannon and Kimchee's office teasing leads to a meaningful discovery for Kimchee.

programme.title: Kim's Convenience: New Appa-liance
programme.desc: Appa's got a plan to get a new appliance for less, Jung looks to mend old fences and Janet searches for a way to differentiate herself online.

programme.title: Kim's Convenience: Cutie Pie
programme.desc: Appa worries Umma will be jealous of a flirty online review. Jung returns to his old stomping grounds. Gerald's girlfriend ruffles Janet's feathers.

programme.title: Kim's Convenience: Open Kimunication
programme.desc: Appa and Umma take their bickering to a new level. Janet crosses paths with a photography idol. Shannon's boyfriend asks Jung for a favor.

programme.title: Kim's Convenience: Thy Neighbour's Wifi
programme.desc: Appa has a solution for spotty Wi-Fi: use the neighbor's. Jung has eyes for Shannon's friend. Umma and Pastor Nina ask Janet to do a church reading.

programme.title: Kim's Convenience: Army Spoon
programme.desc: Gerald photographs Appa and his spoon for an exhibit. Janet's apology for a restaurant faux pas doesn't go well. Umma receives an alarming call.

programme.title: Kim's Convenience: The Kim Cup
programme.desc: An old ping-pong table rekindles a rivalry between Appa and Jung. Umma sets out to bond with Janet but finds Chelsea to be more receptive.

programme.title: Kim's Convenience: Appanoon Delight
programme.desc: Appa's quest to retrieve his drill from Janet leads to an amorous encounter with Umma. Jung gives Kimchee unsolicited advice and plenty of it.

programme.title: Kim's Convenience: To Him It May Concern
programme.desc: Following Appa's spelling error, Umma becomes the store sign maker. Kimchee's mom visits and Janet lands an internship for Gerald.

programme.title: Kim's Convenience: Blabber Talker
programme.desc: Appa and Umma search for an excuse to avoid vacationing with the Lees. Janet scores a lucrative but awkward photography gig.

programme.title: Kim's Convenience: Elephant in the Room
programme.desc: Kimchee gets conflicting dating advice from Jung and Shannon. Janet's outraged when she learns that Umma's sold off her childhood keepsakes.

programme.title: Kim's Convenience: Appanticitis
programme.desc: Appa gives Umma feedback on an annoying habit and promptly regrets it. Janet bumps into an old flame and Jung considers giving reality TV a shot.

programme.title: Kim's Convenience: Hit 'n' Fun
programme.desc: A small fender bender leads to a big argument between Appa and Umma. Jung second-guesses a birthday greeting. Janet goes from mocker to mocked.

programme.title: Kim's Convenience: Lord of the Ring
programme.desc: Umma tries to find her missing wedding ring before Appa does. Jung crashes Janet's yoga class.

programme.title: Kim's Convenience: The Trollop
programme.desc: Only the Mehtas, Janet and Gerald know thus far that Raj cancelled his wedding with only the latter two knowing he did so because he is still in love with Janet.

programme.title: Kim's Convenience: Couch Surfing
programme.desc: Appa is reluctant to have a house guest, but Umma is the one who ends up having problems with their visitor. Jung accuses Janet of being coldhearted and Shannon tackles workplace bullying .

programme.title: Kim's Convenience: The Help
programme.desc: Umma is mistaken for a server at a school event for Janet. Appa takes up math tutoring to prove his teaching prowess. Meanwhile, Shannon is finally single but isn't sure if Jung will make a move.

programme.title: Corporate: Vacation
programme.desc: Vacation: While Matt's off on vacation, Jake is forced to share their office with an unsettling new deskmate. Nihilistic, satirical sitcom from Comedy Central U.S. (S2, E09)

programme.title: Corporate: The Fall
programme.desc: The Fall: Hampton DeVille goes all in on marketing end-of-the-world preparedness, leading Matt to quit and Jake to take on a secret project. Satirical sitcom from Comedy Central U.S. (S2, E10)

programme.title: Corporate: Pickles 4 Breakfast
programme.desc: Hampton DeVille attempts to rewrite the finale of a fan-favourite series, and Jake learns a disturbing truth about a beloved children's show. (S3, E01)

programme.title: Corporate: Black Dog
programme.desc: Matt grows increasingly worried about Jake's bleak outlook on life, and Hampton DeVille brainstorms a new company logo. Bob Odenkirk guests as the voice of Black Dog. (S3, E02)

programme.title: Corporate: The Importance of Talking Sh*t by Oscar Wilde
programme.desc: As the employees of Hampton DeVille gossip and take passive-aggressive approaches to workplace conflicts, they create unlikely bonds and new adversaries. (S3, E03)
programme.sub-title: The Importance of Talking Sh*t by Oscar Wilde

programme.title: Corporate: Good Job
programme.desc: Jake learns the importance of a five-star rating, John desperately seeks Matt's approval, and Grace attempts to come out of her shell. (S3, E04)

programme.title: Corporate: **** You Money
programme.desc: While on a business trip, Jake enjoys the pleasures of a hotel, Matt faces his frugal fears, and they both learn a lesson from a fellow traveller. (S3, E05)

programme.title: Corporate: The Wind of God
programme.desc: John and Kate make a shocking power grab, Christian feels the wrath of a disgruntled former employee, and Jake and Matt try to climb the corporate ladder. Series finale - last ever episode! (S3, E06)

programme.title: Corporate: The Void
programme.desc: The Void: When an insensitive tweet threatens Hampton DeVille's business, Jake and Matt are tasked with firing the employee who posted it. (S1, E01)

programme.title: Corporate: The Powerpoint of Death
programme.desc: The Powerpoint of Death: Matt creates an impressive presentation to pitch Hampton DeVille's arms dealings to the CIA, while Jake takes advantage of the company's internal whistleblower app. (S1, E02)

programme.title: Corporate: The Pain of Being Alive
programme.desc: The Pain of Being Alive: After his surgery, Jake's co-workers butter him up in the hopes that he'll share his prescription painkillers with them, and visions of a ghost haunt Matt. (S1, E03)

programme.title: Corporate: Trademarq
programme.desc: Trademarq: Hampton DeVille partners with a street artist who vandalised the company's headquarters to sell anti-corporate merch and profit off its protesters. (S1, E04)

programme.title: Corporate: Corporate Retreat

programme.title: Corporate: Casual Friday
programme.desc: Casual Friday: Matt and Jake struggle to convince a co-worker to wear a shirt on Casual Friday, and Christian seeks to strike a deal with a group of megachurches. (S1, E06)

programme.title: Corporate: The Long Meeting

programme.title: Corporate: Society Tomorrow
programme.desc: Society Tomorrow: As the staff obsesses over a prestige TV show, Jake resists their attempts to force him to watch, while Matt's life starts to eerily echo the events of the series. (S1, E08)

programme.title: Corporate: Weekend
programme.desc: Weekend: John pressures Jake and Matt into helping him move a grandfather clock in his apartment, sabotaging their weekend plans. (S1, E09)

programme.title: Corporate: Remember Day
programme.desc: Remember Day: Matt copes with his family trauma by throwing himself into the office holiday party, Christian questions his worldview, and Jake considers leaving the company. (S1, E10)

programme.title: Corporate: The One Who's There

programme.title: Corporate: The Concert

programme.sub-title: Football Star Accused: The Alleged Victim Speaks Out Pt.2

programme.sub-title: Russian Hackers Or Lying Wife? Pt. 2

programme.sub-title: My Husband Spanked Me With A Wooden Spoon Pt.1

programme.title: Counting Cars

programme.title: Counting Cars

programme.title: Counting Cars

programme.title: Counting Cars

programme.title: Counting Cars

programme.title: Counting Cars

programme.title: Counting Cars

programme.title: Counting Cars

programme.title: Counting Cars

programme.title: Counting Cars

programme.title: Counting Cars

programme.title: Counting Cars

programme.title: Counting Cars

programme.title: Counting Cars

programme.title: Counting Cars

programme.title: Counting Cars

programme.title: Counting Cars

programme.title: Geordie OGs
programme.desc: Sophie visits Charlotte for advice, Holly visits Charlotte Dawson and gets fired up. Aaron and Talia visit her family. Marnie gets a visit from her wedding planner who brings bad news. (S4, ep4)

programme.title: Geordie OGs
programme.desc: Holly goes ahead with her "warts an' all" Body Confidence photoshoot! Sophie reveals some truths to a life coach. Marnie and Casey have got wedding venue issues. It's Romeo's 1st Birthday! (S4, ep5)

programme.title: Geordie OGs
programme.desc: Holly posts the Body Confidence photos and is noticed by a Kardashian! Aaron's eyeing his first boxing match and Marnie falls in love with a million-pound pad. (S4, ep6)

programme.title: Geordie OGs
programme.desc: Holly turns to the spiritual world for wedding answers. Marnie's million pound home offer is accepted and Talia's waters break prematurely, sparking dramatic events. (S4, ep7)

programme.title: Geordie OGs
programme.desc: Aaron's worried when Talia has to stay in hospital. Sophie embraces single life and Holly's pleased when Jacob meets an Ex-Pro to talk about life after football. (S4, ep8)

programme.title: Geordie OGs
programme.desc: Maddox is home but Talia's struggling post C-Section. Sophie invests in her own beauty business. Marnie's dream house move might be back on and Government travel restrictions impact Holly. (S4, ep9)

programme.title: Geordie OGs
programme.desc: Aaron misses his train to a press conference announcing his big fight, will he make it in time? Holly gets a tattoo removed and Marnie has her baby scan. Gary invites Aaron to meet Chester. (S1, ep1)

programme.title: Geordie OGs
programme.desc: Marnie and Casey do a photo shoot for OK! Magazine. Holly takes a class in public speaking and it's date night for Gary and Emma. The stress of Aaron's upcoming fight is beginning to show. (S1, ep2)

programme.title: Geordie OGs
programme.desc: Marnie gives Casey a sexy birthday present. Holly faces her fears at Sussex Uni. Aaron meets up with Nathan and Sam to talk about mental health. Emma has a big surprise for Gary. (S1, ep3)

programme.title: Geordie OGs
programme.desc: Marnie shows off her baby bump in a magazine shoot. Gary plays golf with Chris Hughes. Aaron's shocked to find out who his opponent is in his MMA fight and Holly says a tearful goodbye. (S1, ep4)

programme.title: Geordie OGs
programme.desc: Gary and Emma share some special news and Aaron gets stuck into training. Holly goes to the House of Lords and Marnie and Casey get a shock when an photo of Casey is sent to a newspaper. (S1, ep5)

programme.title: Geordie Shore
programme.desc: Chloe gets jealous of Holly and Marty and kicks off. Scott receives some big news and there's a shock arrival which has huge consequences for Gaz and Chantelle. (S13, ep4)

programme.title: Geordie Shore
programme.desc: It's seriously awks between Sophie, Gaz and Chantelle. Aaron finally returns but Marnie isn't happy. Chantelle and Marty clash over Chloe. Nathan takes getting mortal to the next level. (S13, ep5)

programme.title: Geordie Shore
programme.desc: It's the morning after Chantelle's departure... Sophie gets some big news from boss Anna, Aaron ends up in an ambulance and Chloe goes all out to get over Marty at the pool party. (S13, ep6)

programme.title: Geordie Shore
programme.desc: Marty ends up in hospital after the beach party. Meanwhile, Boss Anna has some big news for Sophie, Nathan has an unusual plan to help Chloe, and Gaz comes to a decision over Charlotte. (S13, ep7)

programme.title: Geordie Shore
programme.desc: It's Holly's birthday and she gets a massive surprise when Kyle shows up. Marty goes in on Chloe but takes it too far. Meanwhile, Aaron and Marnie's relationship reaches breaking point. (S13, ep8)

programme.title: Geordie Shore
programme.desc: The Geordies reach boiling point... Marty tries to make it up to Chloe, but will she forgive him? And is there any way back for Aaron and Marnie after the previous night's break up? (S13, ep9)

programme.title: Geordie Shore
programme.desc: In the series finale Chloe and Marty get flirty again, but can they really stay just good friends? Kyle gets some big news from Boss Anna, but the fallout will change the family forever. (S13, ep10)

programme.title: Geordie Shore
programme.desc: The Geordies are back! Big Boss Anna shocks the Squad when she sends in some new workers to join them. The lads are excited but Sophie and Chloe aren’t so sure and things quickly go west. (S14, ep1)

programme.title: Geordie Shore
programme.desc: Aaron and Marty decide to get close to newbies Sarah and Abbie. After her kick-off with Zahida, Chloe gets some big news. And the family is stunned when two more new workers arrive. (S14, ep2)

programme.title: Geordie OGs
programme.desc: Marnie could give birth at any moment. Sophie opens up to Holly about her heartbreak. Chester gives Gary the run around and Aaron faces up to the reality of becoming a Dad. (S2, ep1)

programme.title: JAG: Overdue And Presumed Lost
programme.desc: A salvage company tries to claim rights to the wreckage of the USS Dolphin, a WW2 submarine that was lost the day before the attack on Pearl Harbor in 1941.

programme.title: JAG: Killer Instinct
programme.desc: Louis Cellucci is having a rough time aboard the flight deck of the USS Benjamin Harrison. Moments after he walks away from another failed attempt at fixing an F-14, shouts of Man Overboard! are heard across the deck.

programme.title: JAG: To Walk on Wings
programme.desc: Congresswoman Latham and a colleague watch four marine jumpers jump into the ocean from an M22 Osprey. With two marines left to jump, the plane is struck by a swell in the ocean. Harm and Mac are instructed to investigate.

programme.title: JAG: Guilt
programme.desc: When Captain Huddleston hears that the marine corporal has been charged with rape, he sends Lt. Colonel Sarah Mac MacKenzie to handle the case in a hot zone of Anti-American activity.

programme.title: JAG: Mixed Messages
programme.desc: An intruder shoots three Navy people, killing two. One of the dead is a friend of Harm, who protects his reputation. Webb, counter-espionage, and disinformation are involved; Bud solves it. Mac and Renee return to town; Mac leaves again.

programme.title: JAG: Ambush
programme.desc: Capt. Shepard leads his troop off its peacekeeping mission and into battle with the colonel of the rebel forces. Consequently, six marines die in the public incident. Harm is sent in to investigate despite a recent touch of bad luck in a string of trials.

programme.title: JAG: Jag-a-thon
programme.desc: The JAG gang takes part in a JAGathon, a charity-benefit marathon, which provides opportunities for personal pride and individual competitiveness. Sturgis feels uncertain, and Harm defends a sailor for impersonating an officer.

programme.title: JAG: The Mission
programme.desc: A spotter on the ground calls for an airstrike as he has identified 15 heavily armed suspected Al Qaeda members on horseback. JAG won’t approve the airstrike until the spotter is absolutely certain the men are Al Qaeda members.

programme.title: JAG: Defending His Honor
programme.desc: An ambulance pulls up to a crash site where a woman, Jennifer Wilson, is found injured and crying for her baby. Unbeknownst to her, the six-month-old baby died in the accident. A Navy judge stands accused of vehicular homicide.

programme.title: JAG: In Country
programme.desc: Central Command coordinates an airstrike on a confirmed target traveling through Afghanistan. After the strike is launched, marine troops assess the damage and they are disturbed to learn that they targeted the wrong vehicle and hit civilians instead.

programme.title: JAG: Enemy Below
programme.desc: Kabir is preparing an attack against the U.S. and was last seen crossing the border from Afghanistan to Iran. He is believed to have recruited Capt. Yerastov, a Russian submarine captain. Turner, Harm and Mac help in the hunt for the terrorists.

programme.title: JAG: Critical Condition
programme.desc: Bud has lost his right lower leg due to a mine in Afghanistan; during emergency surgery he faces bad trouble. Sturgis returns to Washington, and Harm and Mac disobey an order from the admiral. Bud encounters an astounding change of course.

programme.title: JAG: Dangerous Game
programme.desc: A deputy sheriff dies while chasing a car carrying two SEALs in a Navy exercise. The driver stands trial for negligent homicide.

programme.title: JAG: In Thin Air
programme.desc: Lt. Albrecht and Lt. Cmdr. Zuzello make Zuzello’s farewell flight before he retires. All is fine until Albrecht notices that his partner is passed out from hypoxia. Albrecht makes an emergency landing on the ship.Harm is sent to the ship to investigate.

programme.title: JAG: Need to Know
programme.desc: As a favor from Congresswoman Lillian Dorning, SECNAV Sheffield asks the JAG staff to get the story of a submarine, USS Angel Shark (SSGN-559), which was lost on a CIA mission in 1968.

programme.title: JAG: Friendly Fire
programme.desc: Harm serves as a judge and Mac is the contentious prosecutor in a case fraught withpolitical implications when an American aviator is charged with killing three Britishsoldiers during a flight mission in Afghanistan.

programme.title: JAG: Shifting Sands
programme.desc: When a Gulf War MIA victim is found as the sheik’s wife in a nomadic Arab tribe, Bud prosecutes and Mac defends her against charges of desertion and, in a stunning turn of events, treason. Harm tries to get his old job back but is turned away.

programme.title: JAG: The One That Got Away
programme.desc: Mac defends and Turner prosecutes in the case of a special op marine who, during an Iraqi mission, may have deserted his team for his own survival. Harm takes on a CIA training assignment in a mach six craft that soon has him in danger.

programme.title: JAG: Close Quarters
programme.desc: Back at JAG, Harm is flooded with cases but still wants to help out an 80-year-old veteran accused of robbery. Meanwhile, Turner takes on an assignment in a sub whose crew has rescued possible defecting North Koreans.

programme.title: JAG: Posse Comitatus
programme.desc: Harm and Mac argue the case of a Marine pilot who used force to interfere with a local police matter that led to a man’s killing. Roberts is the investigating officer in the matter of a Navy doctor who is requesting conscientious objector status.

programme.title: Random Breath Test
programme.desc: A reality show on real alcohol and drug testing performed on Australian roads by the Police's RBT Squad.

programme.title: COPS starts at 9pm
programme.desc: Cops gives viewers access into the daily lives and work of the men and women of law enforcement. Filmed on location, the groundbreaking series follows real officers on patrol as they conduct high-speed pursuits, wild arrests and dangerous police business.

programme.title: COPS: Cops Just Hanging Out
programme.desc: A jumpy bicyclist mistakenly reveals more than he had planned, and officers search a darkened building in an attempt to thwart a robbery in progress.

programme.title: COPS
programme.desc: In this Las Vegas Special, A woman claims she was driving a stolen car to get to a birthday party. A sovereign citizen is caught with a fake plate. Tourists believe someone is hiding in their Airbnb.
programme.sub-title: Las Vegas: Fake it til you make it

programme.title: COPS
programme.desc: A man assaults his sister and her dog, a gibberish-speaking man is arrested for attacking his neighbors, and a couple with no-contact orders tells conflicting stories.

programme.title: COPS
programme.desc: Police are led on a lengthy chase, a man is pulled over in a new car he doesn't own, and a detained woman has choice words about her boyfriend.

programme.title: COPS
programme.desc: A burglary results in an act of solidarity, a foot pursuit ends when the K-9 unit shows up, and a person with a substance user disorder leaves a strong impression on an officer.

programme.title: COPS: Cops Brotherly Love
programme.desc: A recently released convict leads police on a high-speed chase in a stolen vehicle, and officers respond to a report of a man pulling a knife on his brother.

programme.title: COPS: Allergy Season
programme.desc: A drunk driver rams into a pursuing police car. A man on a bike flees an area known for drug activity on a bike. A sniffling man attempts to hide a baggie with narcotics under his shirt.

programme.title: COPS: Cops Love Gone Bad
programme.desc: A man on a dirt bike tries to elude police while dumping his firearm, and after breaking up a parking lot drug deal, an officer advises a young suspect to make better choices.

programme.title: COPS
programme.desc: A fleeing motorcyclist crashes into an occupied trailer, a stubborn suspect plays dumb, and officers are disappointed when they run into a repeat offender.

programme.title: COPS: Maybe it's Sugar
programme.desc: Police conduct a sting operation. A woman is covered in white powder during a traffic stop. Officers spot a young boy on a bike who wandered away from home.

programme.title: COPS
programme.desc: A man tries to jump out of a car when his friend takes him on a high-speed chase, and a deputy investigates a man he finds walking down a dark road.

programme.title: COPS
programme.desc: Officers use a police SUV and a taser to stop a fleeing man with a knife. A hit-and-run suspect crashes into a gas station. An unlicensed driver has warrants in Greene County, Missouri.

programme.title: COPS
programme.desc: Stop Sticks are used to end the pursuit of a man that Deputies have previously arrested. Two ghost guns are recovered during a traffic stop. Sisters argue over access to their elderly mother.

programme.title: COPS: Cops Dishonest Dudes
programme.desc: Torrential rain hampers the pursuit of a fleeing vehicle, a man on a moped is carrying more than he lets on, and a persistent police officer unravels a sketchy man's story.

programme.title: COPS: Dangerous Toppings
programme.desc: A jaywalker with no id bolts away from an officer. A man mixes illicit items with his pizza. A woman involved in a domestic violence call blames her monthly issues.

programme.title: COPS: Cops Sittin' in the Dark
programme.desc: A suspect seen fleeing a vehicle refuses to admit he was ever in said vehicle, and police respond to a call that shots were fired.

programme.title: COPS
programme.desc: A bachelorette party is rescued from a sinking boat. A group of spring breakers is forced to pour out liquor. A man is caught selling beer from a backpack. A boiled peanut vendor drifts toward danger.

programme.title: COPS
programme.desc: Officers smell the pungent odor of marijuana during a minor traffic violation. Cocaine is found on a suspect pulled over for a broken windshield. A store is robbed by someone in a red dress.

programme.title: COPS: Cops Bikini Brawl
programme.desc: Officers are called when a mother's job hunt takes a turn for the worse, and deputies confront a nervous man with a lot of bad habits.

programme.title: Random Breath Test
programme.desc: A reality show on real alcohol and drug testing performed on Australian roads by the Police's RBT Squad.

programme.title: The Love Boat: Gopher's Bride / Workaholic / On Second Thought
programme.desc: Gopher and Doc battle for the affections of Gopher's gorgeous pen pal (Marie Laurin) who has come to live with him.
programme.sub-title: Gopher's Bride / Workaholic / On Second Thought

programme.title: The Love Boat: Lose One, Win One / For The Record / Mind My Wife
programme.desc: Doc has his arms full when he agrees to show his best friend's amorous wife (Jill St. John) a good time.
programme.sub-title: Lose One, Win One / For The Record / Mind My Wife

programme.title: The Love Boat: Bugged / Eye Of The Beholder / Lady From Sunshine Gardens
programme.desc: A nudist (Barbi Benton) gets help from an attorney (Peter Haskell) when she wants to bare it all on board.
programme.sub-title: Bugged / Eye Of The Beholder / Lady From Sunshine Gardens

programme.title: The Love Boat: Two For Julie / Aunt Hilly / The Duel
programme.desc: Academy Award-winning actress Olivia de Havilland stars as the captain's aunt, who may take away his daughter, Vicki, when she boards with her husband (Joseph Cotten).
programme.sub-title: Two For Julie / Aunt Hilly / The Duel

programme.title: The Love Boat: Love With A Skinny Stranger / Vicki And The Gambler / That Old Gang Of Mine
programme.desc: Capt. Stubing becomes concerned about his daughter, Vicki, when his gambler friend (Gene Barry) has her calling the long shots.
programme.sub-title: Love With A Skinny Stranger / Vicki And The Gambler / That Old Gang Of Mine

programme.title: The Love Boat: The Model Marriage / Too Clothes For Comfort / Original Sin / This Year's Model / Vogue Rogue - Part 1
programme.desc: Dazzling high fashion, romance, intrigue, and comedy reign on beautiful seas as the Love Boat sails with a star-studded guest cast on a thrilling cruise to Acapulco.
programme.sub-title: The Model Marriage / Too Clothes For Comfort / Original Sin / This Year's Model / Vogue Rogue - Part 1

programme.title: The Love Boat: Lost And Found / Then There Were Two / Maid For Each Other
programme.desc: Gopher's wealthy aunt (Jane Powell) meets a wealthy man (Howard Keel) and is afraid someone may discover her heartbreaking secret.
programme.sub-title: Lost And Found / Then There Were Two / Maid For Each Other

programme.title: The Love Boat: Tony And Julie / Separate Beds / America's Sweetheart
programme.desc: Julie falls hopelessly in love with a handsome doctor (Anthony Andrews).
programme.sub-title: Tony And Julie / Separate Beds / America's Sweetheart

programme.title: The Love Boat: The Expedition Part 2 / The Mongala/ Julie's Replacement/ The Three R's/ Professor's Wife - Part 2
programme.desc: Capt. Stubing walks Julie down the aisle for her marriage to Tony, the handsome Australian doctor who proposed to her at the end of Season 4.
programme.sub-title: The Expedition Part 2 / The Mongala/ Julie's Replacement/ The Three R's/ Professor's Wife - Part 2

programme.title: The Love Boat: Return Of The Captains Lady / Love Ain't Illegal/ The Irresistible Man
programme.desc: Capt. Stubing considers getting remarried while an unscrupulous businessman leaves his girlfriend on the ship with his shy partner. Plus, a man wreaks havoc while trying to attract the attention of a woman, much to the chagrin of another female passenger.
programme.sub-title: Return Of The Captains Lady / Love Ain't Illegal/ The Irresistible Man

programme.title: The Love Boat: The Lady From Laramie / Vicki Swings/ Phantom Bride
programme.desc: A provincial woman finds herself hopelessly in love with a paid escort, while Gopher sees the ghost of a man and wife still occupying a room aboard the ship.
programme.sub-title: The Lady From Laramie / Vicki Swings/ Phantom Bride

programme.title: The Love Boat: Chef's Special / Kleinschmidt/ Beginning Anew
programme.desc: A beautiful, fun-loving woman with a secret (Joan Fontaine) reunites with a former love (Richard Basehart) aboard the ship. Plus, an inept German detective almost turns the Pacific Princess upside down when his employer's jewels are stolen.

programme.title: The Love Boat: Love Honor And Obey / Gladys And Agnes/ Radioactive Isaac
programme.desc: As widow tries to set up her spinster sister with an English lord, Isaac's overtures to a beautiful girl fall apart when his new tooth filling starts receiving strange transmissions.n may lead to divorce.
programme.sub-title: Love Honor And Obey / Gladys And Agnes/ Radioactive Isaac

programme.title: The Love Boat: Isaac The Marriage Counselor / The Incredible Hunk/Jewels And Jim
programme.desc: The ship shudders when an entertainer hired by Julie turns out to be an exotic dancer and he doesn't want his secret revealed. Meanwhile, Isaac learns that a former passenger misunderstood his advice and divorced his wife.
programme.sub-title: Isaac The Marriage Counselor / The Incredible Hunk/Jewels And Jim

programme.title: The Love Boat: Take A Letter Vicky / The Floating Bridge Game/ The Joy Of Celibacy
programme.desc: An emotionally cold woman finds herself falling for a Romeo with hot ideas. Meanwhile, a widow's romance with an attractive dentist is hindered by her nosy, card-playing companions (Rose Marie, Jan Clayton, and Lucille Benson).
programme.sub-title: Take A Letter Vicky / The Floating Bridge Game/ The Joy Of Celibacy

programme.title: The Love Boat: The Viking's Son / Love Boat - Pride Of The Pacific/ Separate Vacations/ The Experiment - Part 2
programme.desc: An emotionally cold woman finds herself falling for a Romeo with hot ideas. Meanwhile, a widow's romance with an attractive dentist is hindered by her nosy, card-playing companions (Rose Marie, Jan Clayton, and Lucille Benson).
programme.sub-title: The Viking's Son / Love Boat - Pride Of The Pacific/ Separate Vacations/ The Experiment - Part 2

programme.title: The Love Boat: Good Neighbors / Captain's Portrait/ Familiar Faces
programme.desc: Capt. Stubing develops an unusual relationship with a lovely woman who is tasked with painting his portrait.
programme.sub-title: Good Neighbors / Captain's Portrait/ Familiar Faces

programme.title: Melrose Place: Full Metal Betsy
programme.desc: Michael and Amanda hit the road in search of Peter and Kimberly. Jane blackmails Sydney into participating in her plot to murder Richard.

programme.title: Melrose Place: Dear Sisters Walking
programme.desc: Peter is saved from the lobotomy when Michael and Amanda break into the hospital, but "Betsy" captures them and locks them in the boiler room. Peter is arrested for Bobby's murder.

programme.title: Melrose Place: Living With Disaster
programme.desc: Richard torments Jane's conscience by leaving clues about the murder around her home and office. Detective Wylie tells Amanda that Peter is actually Dr. Peter Howell, who is wanted in Kansas City on charges of murdering his wife.

programme.title: Melrose Place: Over Dick's Dead Body
programme.desc: Jane suspects that Richard is still alive. Sydney suggests that they dig up the body to alleviate her sister's fears.

programme.title: Melrose Place: Moving Violations
programme.desc: Jane and Sydney escape when Jane hits Richard in the gut with a shovel. As a deputy is driving Jane and Syd to meet Samantha, they see Richard come out of a grocery store. Richard is killed in a shootout.

programme.title: Melrose Place: Hunka, Hunka Burnin Love

programme.title: Melrose Place: Un-Janed Melody
programme.desc: Jane saves Jake and Alison from the fire. Jane apologizes for her outburst but later tries to seduce Billy to make the couple jealous.

programme.title: Melrose Place: Jane's Addiction

programme.title: Melrose Place: Young Doctors in Heat
programme.desc: Amanda and Peter continue to fight constantly. Peter decides to stay with Michael and Kimberly for a while.

programme.title: Melrose Place: Mission Interpersonal

programme.title: Melrose Place: Farewell Mike's Concubine
programme.desc: Kimberly orders Megan to stop "servicing" Michael, but he refuses to stay away from her. Michael cuts off Megan's relationship with her clients.

programme.title: Melrose Place: Nice Work If You Can Get It

programme.sub-title: Nice Work If You Can Get It

programme.title: Melrose Place: Sole Sister

programme.title: Melrose Place: Quest for Mother
programme.desc: Kimberly rejects Michael's final attempt at a reconciliation. He decides to stay with Matt for a while, but Kimberly urges Megan to get Michael back. Dan pressures Matt to move in with him.

programme.title: Melrose Place: Crazy Love

programme.title: Melrose Place: The Accidental Doctor

programme.title: Melrose Place: Escape from L. A.

programme.title: Melrose Place: The Eyes of the Storm
programme.desc: Michael insists on spending time with Kimberly after learning of her terminal illness. She reveals to Megan that she will crash her car during the next rainstorm.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Tubbies crawl through the arch in Home Hill and watch Alex Pascall and some children sing 'Handy Hands'. A pair of mittens appear in Teletubbyland and Tinky Winky does a special dance wearing them.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies pop up from behind the hills to say “Eh-oh!” before watching three children and Andy Brown look in Rock pools for shrimps. Tinky Winky, Dipsy & Laa-Laa look for Po.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch a little boy whose Dad is a firefighter. A beautiful fountain appears and Laa-Laa sings a fountain song. The fountain plays music like Laa-Laa’s song.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch Andy Brown and some children looking on a beach for Hermit Crabs. The Teletubbies love to play games. Today, they are playing a coming and going game.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Two children are making Christmas crackers for each other. Dipsy is very pleased to find his present is a cracker. He looks for someone to pull the cracker.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch two girls go ten pin bowling. Laa-Laa is playing with her ball. It begins to rain, so Laa-Laa plays with her ball indoors – not very carefully.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies dance a twisty dance before watching Alex Pascall sing 'Little Baby'. Tinky Winky, Dipsy and Laa-Laa decide to go for a walk, but Po is still asleep. They sing and clap their hands to try and wake up Po.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies are feeling tired. Andy Brown and a little girl look for spiders in a garden. Two voice trumpets rise and tell Tinky Winky the nursery rhyme 'Incy Wincy Spider'.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Andy Brown and two children watch a robin in the garden. The Teletubbies are 'Calling Out!' Laa-Laa calls to Dipsy who is very far away, then they call to Tinky Winky.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies laugh as they bump tummies. Then they watch some children play a body to body game. The Teletubbies love to play, they play a falling down game.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch some children learn about the colour orange. Laa-Laa loves her ball, she chases it all over Teletubbyland announcing to Tinky Winky, Dipsy and Po that her ball is orange.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch two children take their dog for a walk. A voice trumpet appears and barks like a dog. Dipsy, Laa-Laa and Po laugh as they run all over Teletubbyland barking.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies play a slide game before watching Becky and Jed look for eggs on their farm. Then their mum cooks them for their tea.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch James help his mummy put his baby brother, Angus to bed. In Teletubbyland, Dipsy is asleep, so the other Teletubbies are being quiet.

programme.desc: The Teletubbies see three children at a Mandir Temple where they are celebrating the Diwali festival. The Teletubbies have a very busy day. They watch the clouds, smell the flowers, look for rabbits and run round the house, but they are still not tired.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Tinky Winky brings the other Teletubbies out of the house to watch Andy Brown and two children look at caterpillars. A book appears in Teletubbyland and shows Po how to count to three.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Po remembers to snow. The Tubbies watch an international Christmas insert. The Teletubbies explore Teletubbyland; they are very excited when they discover that the house, trees and everything is covered in snow.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: A voice trumpet rises and makes the sound of a whistle; Tinky Winky laughs when he hears the sound. The Tubbies watch some children who march and twirl their batons to music.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Tinky Winky makes some adjustments on the switch panel. He joins the other Tubbies to watch a little girl exploring a harp and making music.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies slide in to Home Hill and run off to see a parade celebrating the Chinese New Year. A little boy shows us the dragons. A tooter appears in Teletubbyland and when Tinky Winky blows it very hard it knocks Dipsy's hat off.

programme.title: Teletubbies

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch two children making music using saucepans. Tinky Winky hears a noise coming from inside the house. He goes inside to find Po making Tubby Custard.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: A group of children are showing us their park where they play. A see-saw appears in Teletubbyland. Dipsy and Laa-Laa play on the see-saw.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Tubbies watch some children are playing football in the park. Laa-Laa and Po love playing with the ball. While they play they sing a song about the ball.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Laa-Laa fetches the others to watch a little girl called Catherine play with her toy farm. A magical new event happens in Teletubbyland – Bo Peep has lost her sheep and doesn’t know where to find them.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Andy Brown and some children visit and aquarium to learn how things swim in the water. The Teletubbies play an in and out game when a cloud appears in Teletubbyland.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies walk across Teletubbyland to watch some children paint some pictures in a very interesting and messy way. It’s time for Tubby Custard, but Po makes too much Tubby Custard and the custard goes all over Home Hill. Noo Noo tidies up.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies dance a figure of 8 round the hills and then watch as some children visit a pier. The children look at the sea and watch some men fishing from the end of the pier.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: A voice trumpet rises and makes the Teletubbies laugh. They watch some children exploring the colour green. The Teletubbies love green.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: A voice trumpet appears and the Teletubbies dance, before watching two girls do some Flamenco dancing with their teacher. It's time for the Teletubbies to wash their feet. They love using their Tubby Sponges.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Po makes some adjustments on the switch panel and then joins the others to watch Andy Brown and two children looking in a pond for frogs. Laa-Laa is looking at the flowers, but Dipsy keeps getting in the way.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Po loves jumping. She jumps inside Home Hill, then jumps off to watch some children go blackberry picking. Tinky Winky, Dipsy and Po play a hiding game. Laa-Laa can't see them so she decides to play a game with her ball.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Tinky Winky is looking for the other Teletubbies. He can’t see any of them so he shouts out ‘Big Hug’ and all the Teletubbies come running for a big hug.
